checks.describe: Ollama is running on the host and answers /api/version — the model server the whole lab depends on
checks.run: curl -s --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/version | jq -r 'if .version then "ollama-up" else "ollama-down" end'

checks.describe: the course model qwen3:0.6b is pulled and listed by Ollama
checks.run: ollama list | grep -c 'qwen3:0.6b' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 1 ]; then echo model-present; else echo model-missing; fi'

checks.describe: the Qwen3 tokenizer.json is fetchable from Hugging Face and is a non-trivial file (self-seeds into a temp path, then removes it)
checks.run: T=$(mktemp); curl -sL --max-time 60 -o "$T" https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B/resolve/main/tokenizer.json; SZ=$(wc -c < "$T"); rm -f "$T"; if [ "$SZ" -gt 100000 ]; then echo tokenizer-ok; else echo tokenizer-small; fi

checks.describe: the tokenizer playground runs end to end and prints both the token-count table and the KV-cache table (self-seeds tokenizer.json next to the script, leaves it in place for the learner)
checks.run: cd labs/m1 && { [ -f tokenizer.json ] || curl -sL --max-time 60 -o tokenizer.json https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B/resolve/main/tokenizer.json; }; uv run --with tokenizers tokens.py 2>&1 | grep -E 'Token counts|KV-cache memory|bytes/token' | wc -l | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 3 ]; then echo playground-ok; else echo playground-incomplete; fi'

checks.describe: the OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint returns a non-empty assistant message — the course-long API contract works on the learner's endpoint (self-contained request)
checks.run: curl -s --max-time 60 http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1/chat/completions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"model":"qwen3:0.6b","stream":false,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Reply with the single word ok. /no_think"}]}' | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content | if (. | length) > 0 then "contract-ok" else "contract-empty" end'

checks.describe: the /api/generate response carries the per-phase timing fields the lab instruments (prompt_eval_count/duration for prefill, eval_count/duration for decode) — the instrument itself is live
checks.run: curl -s --max-time 60 http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate -d '{"model":"qwen3:0.6b","prompt":"Say hello. /no_think","stream":false,"think":false}' | jq -r 'if (.prompt_eval_count != null and .prompt_eval_duration != null and .eval_count != null and .eval_duration != null) then "metrics-present" else "metrics-missing" end'

checks.describe: a long-prompt one-word-answer request spends the dominant share of its time in prefill (prefill duration exceeds decode duration) — the prefill-heavy contrast run behaves as designed (self-contained, no run-specific number asserted)
checks.run: P=$(printf 'Runbook step: drain traffic, scale the deployment to zero, wait for pods to terminate, then scale back up and verify health. %.0s' $(seq 1 8)); curl -s --max-time 90 http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate -d "{\"model\":\"qwen3:0.6b\",\"prompt\":\"${P} Based only on the runbook, answer in ONE word: what do you scale to first? /no_think\",\"stream\":false,\"think\":false}" | jq -r 'if (.prompt_eval_duration > .eval_duration) then "prefill-dominates" else "decode-dominates" end'

checks.describe: with thinking on, the same one-word question generates strictly more output tokens than with thinking off — the cost-of-reasoning exhibit is reproducible (self-contained; asserts the shape 'more', never a specific count)
checks.run: OFF=$(curl -s --max-time 60 http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate -d '{"model":"qwen3:0.6b","prompt":"What colour is a clear daytime sky? Answer in one word.","stream":false,"think":false}' | jq '.eval_count'); ON=$(curl -s --max-time 90 http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate -d '{"model":"qwen3:0.6b","prompt":"What colour is a clear daytime sky? Answer in one word.","stream":false,"think":true}' | jq '.eval_count'); if [ "$ON" -gt "$OFF" ]; then echo thinking-costs-more; else echo thinking-not-more; fi

checks.describe: the X-Ray Tokens lens serves its page locally with a same-origin Ollama proxy (self-seeds: starts serve.py in the background on a spare port, curls the root, then stops it)
checks.run: PORT=8017 python3 labs/tools/xray/serve.py >/tmp/m1-xray.log 2>&1 & SP=$!; for i in $(seq 1 20); do curl -s --max-time 2 http://127.0.0.1:8017/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 0.5; done; BODY=$(curl -s --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8017/ 2>/dev/null); kill $SP 2>/dev/null; echo "$BODY" | grep -qi 'x-ray' && echo xray-serves || echo xray-nofile
